name: Subscriber List
description: Fan/subscriber management table with filtering, search, sorting, and bulk actions. Export and messaging capabilities.

standard.description: Full data table with all subscriber info

compact.description: Responsive card grid view with subscriber info cards optimized for mobile and touch interactions

visual_brief: Data table of subscribers/fans with columns: checkbox (for bulk select), avatar, display name, email, subscription tier badge, joined date, status badge (active, cancelled, paused), and a kebab menu for actions. A header bar contains search input, tier filter dropdown, status filter, and bulk action buttons (message, export). A pagination bar at the bottom shows page controls and total count. The compact preset removes the avatar column and reduces row height.

responsive.mobile: Table becomes a card list — each subscriber as a card with avatar, name, tier, and status. Bulk actions move to a bottom action bar. Filters collapse to a filter button.
responsive.tablet: Table with horizontal scroll. Search and primary filters visible. Checkboxes and bulk actions functional.
responsive.desktop: Full table with all columns visible. Filters, search, and bulk actions in a single toolbar row.

layout_hints.list_priority: Subscriber identity, tier, and lifecycle state should be easy to scan before bulk actions or export workflows enter the picture.
layout_hints.selection_discipline: Bulk action controls should only rise in prominence once selection exists. Until then, the table remains the primary surface.
layout_hints.card_translation: Card mode should preserve the same subscriber hierarchy as the table so mobile users do not lose account or tier context.

motion.micro: Selected rows, status badges, and bulk actions may brighten subtly to confirm state changes.
motion.transitions: Filtering, sorting, and pagination should preserve orientation and list trust, especially for managers working through large subscriber sets.

keyboard: Search, filters, row selection, and per-subscriber actions should be reachable in a clear operational order.
announcements: Subscriber name, tier, joined date, and status should be exposed semantically so both table and card variants remain understandable without relying on badge styling alone.

composition.SubscriberList: Container(d-section, flex-col, gap-4) > [Toolbar + SubscriberTable + Pagination + BulkActions?]
composition.SubscriberRow: Row(d-data-row) > [Checkbox(d-control) + Avatar + Name(font-medium) + Email(text-muted) + TierBadge(d-annotation) + JoinDate + StatusBadge(d-annotation) + ActionsMenu(d-interactive)]
